content.problemStatement:
Ezio can manipulate **at most** XXX number of guards with the apple of eden.

Given that there are YYY number of guards, predict if he can safely manipulate all of them.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, two integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, print YES\texttt{YES}YES if it is possible for Ezio to manipulate all the guards. Otherwise, print NO\texttt{NO}NO.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YeS\texttt{YeS}YeS, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, yes\texttt{yes}yes and YES\texttt{YES}YES will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y, where X is the maximum number of guards Ezio can manipulate and Y is the total number of guards.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if X ≥ Y, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: X=5, Y=7 → 5<7 so NO.\nTest case 2: X=6, Y=6 → 6≥6 so YES.\nTest case 3: X=9, Y=1 → 9≥1 so YES.
X equals Y, therefore Ezio can manipulate all guards.
X=10 is less than Y=20, so it is impossible to manipulate all guards.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to compare the two given integers. If the first integer X (maximum manipulable guards) is greater than or equal to the second integer Y (total guards) then Ezio can handle all guards, so we print YES; otherwise we print NO. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        x = int(data[idx]); y = int(data[idx+1]); idx += 2
        out.append('YES' if x >= y else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
